segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of a sizzling pan of spicy Korean-style stir-fry, likely dakdoritang or a similar dish, with steam rising and a hand pouring sauce over it. The scene quickly transitions to a man in a blue shirt with the text 'i travel for food' printed on it, holding a plate of fried meat, possibly pork belly or chicken, as he prepares to eat. The setting shifts to a bustling street with a green bus and Korean signage, indicating a South Korean location. The video then cuts to a dramatic shot of a wok being tossed over an open flame, followed by an aerial view of a rural landscape with roads and mountains, suggesting a journey. Back in a kitchen, a bowl of spicy noodles with vegetables and meat is shown, then a close-up of a fire pit with burning logs, possibly for grilling. The man reappears, now in a blue shirt, sitting at a table with a large wok of food, eating with chopsticks and smiling. The scene changes to a restaurant setting where he, now in an orange shirt, is seated at a table laden with various Korean dishes, including grilled meats, side dishes, and a large platter of spicy stir-fry. He takes a bite of a large piece of meat, clearly enjoying it. The video continues with a close-up of a hand holding a piece of meat with noodles, followed by a shot of a large pan of stir-fried meat and vegetables being cooked. The man, now in a black shirt, is seen in a kitchen setting, eating from a wok with a spoon, surrounded by side dishes. The final scenes show him in an orange shirt again, eating a large piece of grilled meat with chopsticks, and then a close-up of a hand holding a piece of meat with noodles, emphasizing the rich, saucy texture. The video ends with a shot of a large platter of spicy stir-fry, highlighting the vibrant colors and textures of the dish.

segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of a lavish seafood platter featuring lobster, scallops, and shrimp, set on a wooden table surrounded by side dishes, suggesting a rich dining experience. The scene quickly transitions to a close-up of sizzling grilled meat, likely beef ribs, being cooked on a grill, emphasizing the savory textures and juices. The setting then shifts to a car interior, showing a view of a road with greenery and mountains, indicating travel. Inside the car, a man wearing a blue t-shirt with the text 'i travel for food' is seen holding a small bottle, possibly a condiment, and smiling as he opens it. The scene cuts to a kitchen where the same man, now in a black t-shirt with the same text, is enthusiastically lifting a large portion of a spicy stir-fry dish from a wok, with steam rising and side dishes visible in the background. The video then displays a map of South Korea with a red pin marking Incheon, followed by a shot of the Incheon International Airport arrival hall, where travelers are waiting near a large digital flight board. The man, now in a black t-shirt, appears in a selfie-style shot, smiling and speaking to the camera, with the airport’s bustling environment behind him. On-screen text identifies him as 'Mark Wiens, Full Time Eater,' establishing his role as a food-focused traveler. The sequence captures a dynamic journey from indulgent food experiences to travel and arrival, centered around the man’s passion for food exploration.

segments.caption: The video opens with Mark Wiens, Full Time Eater, in a selfie-style shot inside the Incheon International Airport arrival hall, smiling and speaking to the camera. He is wearing a black t-shirt with the text 'i travel for food' and a backpack, standing in a bustling terminal with digital flight boards and travelers in the background. The scene transitions to the exterior of the airport, where he stands on a sidewalk as a silver van pulls up. He gestures excitedly as passengers unload luggage from the van, including a large suitcase. The camera then cuts to a point-of-view shot from inside the van, driving on a highway with a green road sign indicating 'Anyang 42km' and 'Songdo 15km' in Korean and English. The journey continues with an aerial view of a coastal cityscape, showcasing dense urban buildings and the sea. The video then shifts to a street-level view as Mark walks along a sidewalk in a Korean city, passing by a restaurant named '제주은희네 해장국' (Jeju Eunhee's Korean Food) with a red sign and glass front. He continues walking, smiling and gesturing, as other pedestrians pass by, including a woman in a striped shirt. The final shot shows him approaching the restaurant entrance, with a man in a blue shirt walking ahead, suggesting they are heading inside for a meal.

segments.caption: The video begins with an exterior shot of the Korean restaurant '제주은희네 해장국' (Jeju Eunhee's Korean Food), featuring a modern glass facade and red signage, as the Man in blue shirt walks toward the entrance, followed by Mark Wiens, who is now wearing a black t-shirt with Thai text 'ไม่เผ็ด ไม่กิน' (Not Spicy, Not Eating) — a playful twist on his usual 'i travel for food' shirt. Inside, the restaurant has a warm, wooden interior with a large illuminated map of South Korea on the wall and a menu board listing items like '해장국' (hangover soup) and side dishes. The camera pans to a table where Mark Wiens is seated, smiling as a server places a steaming stone pot of Korean soup in front of him. The dish, likely a spicy beef and vegetable stew, is garnished with green onions and red chili flakes, and steam rises as he prepares to eat. He uses chopsticks and a spoon to stir the contents, then lifts a portion of noodles and meat, showing the rich, bubbling broth. The atmosphere is casual and inviting, with other diners visible in the background, and the focus remains on Mark’s enthusiastic interaction with the food, highlighting the textures and steam of the hot dish.

segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of a sizzling Korean barbecue grill, where thick cuts of marbled beef and mushrooms are being expertly flipped with tongs, revealing seared grill marks and rising steam. The scene transitions to a wider shot of a bustling Korean restaurant, where Mark Wiens, wearing a black t-shirt with Thai text 'ไม่เผ็ด ไม่กิน' (Not Spicy, Not Eating), is seated at a table with a built-in grill. He smiles and gestures animatedly as he interacts with the food and his companion, who is also grilling meat. The table is laden with side dishes, including a vibrant red spicy noodle dish, fresh lettuce, and a bowl of mixed greens. Mark assembles a lettuce wrap with grilled meat and vegetables, then takes a satisfying bite, savoring the flavors with expressive enjoyment. He then dips a piece of meat into a small bowl of sauce, chews thoughtfully, and continues to engage with the meal. The camera briefly focuses on a bowl of mixed greens being seasoned with red chili paste, emphasizing the spicy element of the cuisine. The scene concludes with an exterior shot of a quiet Korean street lined with shops and parked cars, suggesting the restaurant’s urban location. Throughout, the restaurant’s industrial-chic interior with exposed ductwork and Korean signage adds to the authentic atmosphere.

segments.caption: The video opens with a man wearing a black t-shirt with Thai text 'ไม่เผ็ด ไม่กิน' (Not Spicy, Not Eating) smiling and speaking directly to the camera on a busy city street in South Korea, with cars and Korean signage visible in the background. He gestures toward the camera and then walks across a crosswalk, transitioning to a wide shot of Yongsan Station, a modern building with glass facades and signage in Korean, English, and Chinese. He enters the station through Entrance 1, where he interacts with a ticket machine, selecting a destination and purchasing a single-use ticket for 1,300 won. Inside the station, he walks through a clean, well-lit concourse, then boards a subway train. He points to a subway map, indicating his destination, and rides the train, smiling at the camera. After exiting the train at Seobinggo station, he walks outside into a bustling street scene, passing a vibrant food stall with rows of rotisserie chickens roasting over open flames, the sign reading '한남동 한방통닭' (Hannam-dong Hanbang Tongdak). The atmosphere shifts from urban transit to a lively street food environment, capturing the essence of Seoul’s dynamic city life.

segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of a large rotisserie oven at a street food stall, where multiple whole chickens are being roasted on rotating spits over an open flame, their skin glistening and turning golden-brown. The camera then pans to reveal Mark Wiens, wearing a black t-shirt with Thai text 'ไม่เผ็ด ไม่กิน' (Not Spicy, Not Eating), standing beside the oven and gesturing excitedly at the chickens, smiling broadly as he speaks to the camera. He points to the different rows of chickens, some more cooked than others, emphasizing the roasting process. The scene transitions to a close-up of the rotating chickens, highlighting their crispy, caramelized skin. The setting then shifts to a small, cozy restaurant where Mark is seated at a wooden table, with a whole roasted chicken placed before him on a foil-lined platter, accompanied by several small bowls of side dishes including kimchi, a red dipping sauce, and a yellow sauce, along with a glass mug of beer. He raises the beer in a toast, smiles, and begins to eat, using chopsticks to tear into the tender, juicy meat of the chicken, revealing its moist interior. The camera focuses on the texture and juiciness of the chicken as he pulls apart the meat, emphasizing the deliciousness of the meal. The on-screen text identifies the location as 'Hannam Oriental Roast Chicken, Seoul, South Korea'. The atmosphere is warm and inviting, with natural lighting and a casual dining setup, capturing the essence of a satisfying street food experience in Seoul.

segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of a whole roasted chicken being expertly carved on a wooden board, its golden-brown skin glistening and tender white meat being pulled apart with a knife and fork. The camera then shifts to reveal Mark Wiens, wearing a black t-shirt with Thai text 'ไม่เผ็ด ไม่กิน' (Not Spicy, Not Eating), seated at a wooden table in a cozy, dimly lit restaurant. He enthusiastically tears off a piece of chicken with his hands, brings it to his mouth, and takes a large bite, his eyes wide with delight. He continues to eat with expressive gestures, savoring the flavor, while the table is set with various Korean side dishes: a bowl of kimchi, a small dish of red sauce, a bowl of yellow dipping sauce, a bowl of white radish, and a large bowl of white rice. A tall glass of beer sits beside him, and a wooden tissue box is visible on the table. The camera occasionally zooms in on the chicken, revealing rice stuffed inside the cavity, and on the side dishes, including a close-up of the white radish being picked up with chopsticks. The atmosphere is warm and intimate, emphasizing the enjoyment of a hearty, traditional meal at Hannam Oriental Roast Chicken.

segments.caption: The video opens in a bustling kitchen where a chef, wearing a blue apron and gloves, prepares a spicy Korean stew in a metal pot. The chef adds layers of ingredients: first, a base of red kimchi, then sliced white radish, followed by chopped green onions, and finally, cubes of tofu. The pot is placed over a high flame, and broth is poured in, causing the stew to bubble vigorously. The scene transitions to a wider kitchen view, showing multiple pots simmering on a gas stove, with a woman in a red shirt and green apron actively cooking. The camera then cuts to a dining area where a man in a black t-shirt with the text 'i travel for food' is seated at a wooden table, surrounded by two steaming pots of the freshly made stew, side dishes including kimchi, a plate of egg omelet, and small bowls of dipping sauces. He enthusiastically stirs the stew with a spoon, takes a bite, and reacts with visible delight, nodding and gesturing as he speaks to the camera. The restaurant’s interior is warm and casual, with wooden tables and a visible kitchen in the background where staff continue to work. The man’s expressive enjoyment highlights the rich, spicy flavor of the dish, emphasizing the communal and satisfying nature of the meal.

He picks up a metal lid and, using a black marker, writes 'The Best Kimchi-Jjigae Thank You! Mark Wiens' on it, then proudly holds it up to the camera with a smile. The video then cuts to a first-person view from inside a moving car, driving through city streets and past buildings, before arriving at a location marked 'Pyongyang Myeonok' in Seoul, South Korea. The camera pans up to show the building's exterior, then moves inside to reveal a narrow, industrial-style corridor with exposed pipes and fans, where people are waiting in line. The man in the black t-shirt is seen looking at a menu board on the wall, gesturing as if making a decision, while others wait nearby. The atmosphere is casual and lively, with a mix of modern and rustic elements in the restaurant’s design.

segments.caption: The video opens with a dynamic cityscape of Seoul, South Korea, showcasing heavy traffic on a multi-lane road with buses, cars, and motorcycles moving under a clear sky, framed by modern high-rises and older buildings. The camera then transitions to a bustling street-level view, panning quickly past colorful storefronts with Korean signage, including a shop with a large display of colorful balloons and another with a blue awning and glass front. The scene shifts to a narrow alleyway lined with small shops and parked vehicles, with overhead wires crisscrossing above. The camera then moves inside a traditional Korean restaurant, 'Woo Lae Oak,' where a man in a black t-shirt with 'i travel for food' printed on it greets the camera with a smile and a wave. The interior features warm wooden decor, a wall of framed certificates, and a central table with a hot grill. The man is seated at the table, gesturing as he speaks, while a server places marinated beef onto the grill. The camera focuses on the sizzling beef as it cooks, with side dishes arranged around the table, capturing the authentic dining experience in a cozy, well-lit restaurant setting.

segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of marbled beef sizzling on a copper mesh grill embedded in a wooden table, with flames flickering beneath and steam rising as tongs flip and stir the meat. The camera then pulls back to reveal the Man in black shirt with 'i travel for food' seated at the table, smiling and gesturing enthusiastically toward the camera, his black t-shirt with white text clearly visible. Around him are various Korean side dishes including kimchi, pickled radish, and fresh greens. The restaurant has a warm, wooden interior with other diners visible in the background. The man continues to interact with the camera, waving and speaking, while the grill remains the focal point. The camera returns to close-ups of the beef being turned, emphasizing its juicy, caramelized texture. A hand then uses chopsticks to place a piece of the cooked beef onto a fresh lettuce leaf, followed by a dollop of red spicy sauce from a small white bowl, assembling a traditional Korean lettuce wrap. The sequence highlights the interactive and communal nature of Korean barbecue dining, with the man’s expressive engagement underscoring his enjoyment of the meal.

segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of a man in a black t-shirt with 'i travel for food' printed on it, wearing sunglasses and smiling broadly while riding in a moving vehicle, waving at the camera. The scene transitions to a view from inside the car, showing a highway with traffic and a green road sign indicating directions to Anyang and Songdo. The camera then captures a sweeping aerial view of a coastal cityscape with colorful rooftops and dense urban development, followed by a map highlighting Yesan-gun in South Korea. The journey continues through city streets at sunset, with the sun casting a golden glow over buildings and vehicles. The man arrives at a market area, where a sign reads 'Welcome to Yesan Daily Market,' and the camera pans to a large display of red chili peppers. Inside a bustling food market, the man is seen in a kitchen, smiling and gesturing as a chef in a blue shirt and green cap cooks in a wok, tossing ingredients like onions and chili peppers. The man appears excited and engaged, interacting with the camera while the chef prepares a dish, likely a spicy stir-fry, with visible steam and sizzling sounds. The setting is lively, with other diners and food stalls visible in the background, emphasizing the vibrant local food culture of Yesan.

segments.caption: The video segment opens with a close-up of a large black wok on a high-flame gas stove, where chopped onions, garlic, and red chili peppers are being sautéed in oil. The chef, wearing black gloves, adds pieces of chicken to the wok, which sizzle and begin to brown. The heat intensifies as the chef skillfully tosses the ingredients, causing a dramatic burst of flames that engulf the wok in a fiery flare-up, showcasing the high-heat cooking technique. After the flames subside, a dark, rich sauce is poured into the wok, coating the chicken and aromatics, followed by a splash of liquid that creates a bubbling, simmering stew. The chef then adds pre-cut white radish and green vegetables from a metal tray, stirring them into the simmering mixture. As the dish thickens, the chef adds a generous amount of chopped green onions and a vibrant green chili paste, mixing everything together with a large metal spoon. The final product is a glossy, spicy stir-fry with tender chicken, vegetables, and a deep red sauce, steaming hot and ready to serve. Throughout the cooking process, the man in the black shirt with 'i travel for food' printed on it appears intermittently, smiling and gesturing enthusiastically at the camera, clearly engaged and excited by the cooking demonstration. The kitchen environment is professional, with stainless steel surfaces, multiple burners, and a visible ventilation hood, indicating a commercial or restaurant setting. The lighting is bright and focused on the wok, emphasizing the sizzling ingredients and the dynamic cooking action.

The background reveals a quiet, dimly lit alley with Korean signage, including a red circular logo with Korean characters, and occasional passersby, adding to the authentic street food atmosphere. segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of a man in a blue t-shirt with the text 'i travel for food' printed on it, smiling at the camera in a sunlit outdoor setting with trees and a picnic table in the background. The scene quickly transitions to a wide shot of a rural field with mountains in the distance, where two men are working near a freshly dug pit. One man in a red shirt and another in a blue shirt are seen placing logs and kindling into the pit, preparing for a fire. The man in the blue shirt then holds up a large metal tray filled with corn, potatoes, and sweet potatoes, showcasing the ingredients for an underground cooking method. He then crouches inside the pit, gesturing to its size, while a small yellow excavator is visible in the background. The men continue to arrange logs and kindling in the pit, and one of them uses a lighter to ignite the fire, producing smoke. The excavator operator then uses the machine to add more soil and cover the fire, sealing the pit. The man in the blue shirt watches with a smile, clearly excited about the cooking process. The scene captures the rustic, hands-on preparation of a traditional underground meal in a scenic, mountainous rural environment under bright daylight.

He is seen smiling and gesturing, indicating enthusiasm for the activity.

segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of a roaring fire in a pit, with flames licking at a large log, setting the scene for an outdoor cooking process. The camera then pulls back to reveal a rural outdoor setting where a man in a red shirt and another in a blue shirt are working together to prepare a fire pit, assisted by a small yellow excavator. The man in the red shirt, wearing gloves, carefully places logs and kindling into the pit, while the man in the blue shirt, also gloved, adds more fuel and adjusts the stones around the fire. Smoke rises as they cover the fire with soil, sealing it for underground cooking. The scene transitions to a kitchen where a man in a blue shirt with 'i travel for food' printed on it smiles at the camera, surrounded by women preparing food. The camera pans over trays of raw ingredients—corn, potatoes, sweet potatoes, and pork belly—before cutting to an aerial view of a lush, green rural landscape with fields and houses. Back in the kitchen, the man in the red shirt arranges the vegetables on a tray, while the man in the blue shirt carries a tray of food outside. The final shot shows the exterior of a modest house with two large ceramic jars in front, as the man in the red shirt walks toward the door, carrying a tray, while the man in the blue shirt follows with another tray, indicating the start of a communal meal preparation.

segments.caption: In a rural outdoor setting under bright daylight, a group of people collaborates on an underground cooking process. A yellow excavator, operated by a man in a red shirt and hat, digs a pit and later covers it with soil, while smoke rises from the pit as green vegetation is burned inside. A man in a blue shirt and rubber boots, along with another man in a gray shirt and hat, work together to clear the smoldering pit, using shovels and their hands to remove ash and debris. They then uncover a large metal pot buried in the ground, which is steaming hot. The pot is lifted out, revealing a bubbling stew with corn, potatoes, and sweet potatoes. The lid is lifted to reveal the cooked contents, with steam billowing out, indicating a successful underground cooking method. The surrounding environment features trees, open fields, and distant mountains, creating a natural and rustic atmosphere.

segments.caption: The video opens with a digital map of South Korea, highlighting Jeonju-si with a red outline and bold text, setting the geographical context. The scene transitions to an aerial view of Jeonju, showcasing a dense urban landscape with traditional and modern buildings nestled among green hills under a clear blue sky. The camera then cuts to a close-up of a man wearing a blue t-shirt with 'i travel for food' printed on it, standing outdoors in bright sunlight, smiling and gesturing enthusiastically as he speaks to the camera. He is wearing a black backpack and appears to be in a bustling street area with a red and blue bus and pedestrians in the background. The video then shifts to a close-up of a vibrant Korean dish, likely bibimbap, with colorful ingredients including red kimchi, green zucchini, and yellow egg yolks, being mixed in a hot stone bowl. The next scene shows the exterior of a traditional Korean restaurant named 'Hankookjib' (한국집), with ornate tiled roofing and a sign displaying the name in both Korean and English, along with a phone number. Inside the restaurant, the same man in the blue shirt is seated at a wooden table, excitedly gesturing as he speaks, with a menu and chopsticks in front of him. The camera pans to a wall display featuring framed photographs of people dining and a certificate in Korean, indicating the restaurant's heritage and recognition. The video then shows a close-up of a large, steaming bowl of bibimbap, rich with ingredients like beef, vegetables, and egg, being mixed with chopsticks and a spoon. The final scenes include a wall display of metal lids signed by customers, a framed certificate from the Ministry of Culture and Tourism, and a menu board listing 'Traditional Jeonju Cuisine Restaurant' with the specialty 'Jeonju Bibimbap,' emphasizing the restaurant's authenticity and cultural significance. The man continues to interact with the camera, expressing excitement about the food, while the restaurant's interior, with wooden beams and traditional decor, provides a warm, inviting atmosphere.

segments.caption: The video opens with a dramatic close-up of a blowtorch igniting a wood fire beneath a large, black wok, sending bright blue and orange flames licking upwards. The scene shifts to a bustling outdoor cooking setup under a canopy, where a chef in a gray shirt and white gloves operates the wok over the roaring fire. A man in a blue t-shirt with 'i travel for food' printed on it appears excitedly, gesturing toward the flames and the cooking process. The camera focuses on the wok as the chef stirs with a long-handled spoon, creating a cloud of steam. A thick, orange-red sauce is poured from a metal bucket into the wok, spreading across the surface and sizzling intensely. The chef then adds chunks of meat and vegetables from another bucket, mixing them into the bubbling sauce. The flames continue to flare up around the wok, emphasizing the high-heat cooking method. The man in the blue shirt reappears, smiling and pointing at the wok, clearly impressed by the spectacle. The final shots show the wok filled with a vibrant, simmering stew, with flames dancing around its edges, capturing the dynamic and fiery atmosphere of the outdoor cooking experience.

segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of a large black wok over a roaring wood fire, where a vibrant, bubbling red sauce with chicken pieces is being cooked, steam rising intensely. The scene shifts to reveal a chef in a gray shirt and white gloves, wearing a black face mask, skillfully stirring the dish with a long-handled metal spoon. He adds chopped white onions and green scallions from a blue plastic bowl, tossing them into the sizzling wok. The camera zooms in as the ingredients are mixed, showing the glossy, rich sauce coating the chicken and vegetables. The scene transitions to a wider shot of the outdoor cooking area, where multiple woks are set over fires under a covered structure, with colorful umbrellas and tables visible in the background. The video then cuts to a dining table at night, where a man in a blue t-shirt with 'i travel for food' printed on it is seated, smiling and excitedly eating the finished dish. He uses chopsticks to pick up a piece of the saucy chicken, takes a bite, and reacts with visible delight, eyes wide and mouth open in enjoyment. The table is set with various side dishes, including a green soup, kimchi, and a metal bowl of the main spicy stir-fry, all under warm, ambient lighting. segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of a metal bowl where raw chicken pieces are being mixed with a creamy batter using a yellow spatula by a gloved hand. The batter is thick and pale, and the chicken is coated thoroughly. The scene shifts to show a second bowl where a white powdery substance, likely flour or starch, is being added and mixed in, thickening the coating. The camera then cuts to a man in a black t-shirt with 'i travel for food' printed on it, standing in front of a street food stall, smiling and speaking to the camera. The scene returns to the cooking process, where the coated chicken is being carefully transferred into a large, bubbling wok filled with hot oil. The chicken sizzles and turns golden brown as it fries. A metal strainer is used to lift the crispy fried chicken out of the oil, draining excess grease. The camera pans to show a second chef in an orange apron and face mask, also frying chicken in a separate wok, indicating a busy street food stall environment. The man in the black shirt reappears, gesturing excitedly as he speaks, clearly enjoying the cooking process. The setting is a bustling outdoor market in Gwangju, South Korea, with visible signage for 'Yangdong Tonidak' and a menu board listing 'Original fried chicken' and 'Fried chicken mixed with sweet sauce' with prices in Korean won. The atmosphere is lively, with steam rising from the woks and the sound of sizzling oil, capturing the essence of traditional Korean street food preparation.

segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of a metal grill suspended over a roaring fire fueled by dry straw, with thin slices of marinated meat sizzling and smoking as flames lick the grate. The camera shifts to show a man in a black t-shirt with 'travel for food' printed in white, sitting cross-legged beside the fire, smiling and gesturing animatedly as he speaks. He is joined by another man wearing a black cap and face mask, who uses a long metal rod to adjust the grill’s position over the flames. The fire crackles intensely, casting a warm, flickering light on the surrounding dark, rustic environment. The camera alternates between close-ups of the sizzling meat, the dancing flames, and the two men interacting, emphasizing the communal and rustic cooking experience. The setting appears to be a dimly lit, open-air or semi-enclosed space, possibly a rural food stall or traditional cooking area, with straw scattered on the ground and a small table visible in the background. The atmosphere is cozy and intimate, centered around the shared act of grilling over an open fire.

segments.caption: The video opens with a digital map of South Korea, highlighting the region of Sokcho-si with a red outline and bold text, setting the geographical context. The scene transitions to a bustling street in Sokcho, where the Man in black shirt with 'i travel for food' is seen walking and speaking directly to the camera, gesturing enthusiastically as he navigates the lively market area. He passes under a colorful archway labeled 'Sokcho Central Market' and enters the Sokcho Tourist & Fishery Market, a covered indoor market filled with seafood stalls and vibrant displays. Inside, he approaches a food stall with a clean, white counter and glass partition, where workers in white uniforms prepare food behind the counter. He purchases two white takeout boxes labeled '만선 닭강정' (Manseon Dakgangjeong) and '주라이드' (Juraide), which he then opens to reveal a spicy, glossy chicken dish topped with sesame seeds. The atmosphere is lively, with other shoppers and vendors visible in the background, and the lighting is bright and commercial, emphasizing the market's energy and the appeal of the food.

segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of a vibrant, saucy Korean-style fried chicken dish, identified as Dakgangjeong, served in a white takeout box and topped with sliced almonds and green onions. The scene transitions to the Man in black shirt with 'i travel for food' standing inside the bustling Sokcho Central Market, holding the open box of spicy chicken. He gestures enthusiastically towards the camera, speaking with excitement, while the market's bright overhead lights and colorful Korean signage (including '만선 닭강정' and '새롬맛집') illuminate the background. He then picks up a piece of the chicken with his fingers, brings it to his mouth, and takes a bite, savoring it with a satisfied expression. He continues to speak to the camera, gesturing with his free hand, clearly enjoying the food. The camera alternates between close-ups of the glistening, red-sauced chicken and medium shots of the man in the lively market environment, capturing the dynamic atmosphere of the covered market with other shoppers and vendors visible in the background. The man's black t-shirt with the white 'i travel for food' logo is clearly visible, along with his black backpack. The segment emphasizes the sensory appeal of the food and the man's genuine reaction to it within the vibrant market setting.

segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of a golden-brown, crispy Korean rice roll being held with metal tongs, revealing its fluffy rice and savory filling. The scene transitions to a man in a black t-shirt with the text 'i travel for food' seated at a white table in a bright, modern restaurant, enthusiastically eating the rice roll with chopsticks. The table is laden with various Korean dishes, including a steaming stone pot soup, side dishes in small white bowls, and a sizzling plate of rolled food. The man gestures excitedly while speaking to the camera, clearly enjoying the meal. The scene then shifts to an outdoor urban setting, showing a wide street with traffic and buildings, followed by a title card reading 'DAY 08: SOKCHO' over a blurred cityscape. The video then cuts to a bustling street in Sokcho, South Korea, with a large red crab sculpture atop a building labeled 'Port Seafood Market'. The man, now standing outside, points towards the market with a smile, and the camera pans to display a vibrant seafood market with tanks filled with live crabs, shrimp, and other fresh seafood. The man continues to speak animatedly, gesturing towards the market, clearly excited about the culinary adventure ahead. The environment transitions from the indoor dining setting to the lively, colorful outdoor market, emphasizing the journey from eating to exploring fresh ingredients.

segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of a Korean seafood restaurant's menu board, prominently displaying '대게바다' (Daegye Bada) and 'Premium 대게 킹크랩 전문' (Premium King Crab Specialty), along with a phone number and images of various crab dishes. The scene transitions to a large, transparent tank filled with live octopuses, their tentacles moving in the water. The camera then cuts to a man wearing a black t-shirt with the text 'i travel for food' printed on it, seated at a wooden table in a casual restaurant setting. He is eating a meal with chopsticks, holding up a piece of food to the camera with a smile. The table is set with various Korean side dishes, including a bowl of spicy red sauce, a plate of sliced fish, and a small bowl of greens. The man continues to eat and interact with the camera, showing his enjoyment. The scene shifts to a close-up of a black rectangular dish containing a glossy, orange-colored food item, possibly a type of seafood or dumpling, being stirred with chopsticks. The camera then cuts to a man in a yellow shirt and apron, working at a seafood stall. He is handling live octopuses in a tank, lifting one out with his hands and placing it on a metal tray. He then moves to a grill, where he places the octopus on a metal rack over a flame, using red gloves to handle the hot food. The man in the yellow shirt continues to cook the octopus, turning it with tongs and ensuring it is evenly cooked. The camera captures the sizzling sound and the steam rising from the grill. The man in the black 'i travel for food' shirt is shown again, smiling and speaking to the camera, likely commenting on the cooking process. The scene ends with the man in the yellow shirt continuing to cook the octopus, with the camera focusing on the grill and the sizzling food.

segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of a chef in a yellow shirt and red gloves handling a large, bright orange king crab, placing it into a metal tray. The setting is a seafood restaurant kitchen with a blue and white tiled wall and a mural of a ship at sea. The chef then moves to a white cutting board, where he skillfully cuts the crab legs with blue-handled scissors and a knife, separating the meat from the shell. The camera focuses on the precision of the cuts, showing the white crab meat being cleanly extracted. The scene transitions to a dining table where a plate of freshly prepared crab legs and a whole crab are presented alongside a plate of scallops. The Man in black shirt with 'i travel for food' is seated at the table, wearing clear plastic gloves, and begins to eat the crab, using scissors to cut the legs and extract the meat. He is joined by another man in a green shirt, who also starts eating. The atmosphere is casual and lively, with the table set with various side dishes and condiments, and the restaurant’s interior features bright signage and a menu board with Korean text, including '대게바다' (Daegye Bada) and 'Premium 대게 킹크랩 전문' (Premium King Crab Specialty). The camera captures the enjoyment and interaction as they savor the fresh seafood.

segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of a hand wearing a transparent plastic glove, holding a bright orange crab leg, emphasizing its freshness and texture. The camera then pans to a large white plate piled with a whole cooked king crab, its legs splayed out, revealing the rich, white meat inside. The setting is a casual Korean seafood restaurant, with wooden tables, a blue tarp backdrop, and a menu board displaying '대게바다' (Daegye Bada) and 'Premium 대게 킹크랩 전문' (Premium King Crab Specialty). The Man in black shirt with 'i travel for food' is seated at the table, actively engaging with the meal. He uses his hands to break open a crab leg, extracting the succulent meat, and dips it into a greenish-yellow sauce, likely a garlic or ginger-based condiment, before taking a bite with evident enjoyment. He continues to eat with enthusiasm, occasionally gesturing and speaking to the camera, his black t-shirt with the white text 'i travel for food' clearly visible. The camera captures close-ups of the crab’s interior, showing the glistening roe and meat, and the man’s focused, satisfied expressions as he savors the dish. The atmosphere is lively, with other diners and restaurant staff visible in the background, and the lighting is warm and inviting, highlighting the vibrant colors of the food and the man’s animated reactions.

The video then shows a scenic waterfront at dusk, with a view of the harbor and mountains in the background, before returning to a close-up of the king crab dish, highlighting its vibrant orange shell and the abundance of crab legs and meat. The atmosphere is warm and inviting, with the restaurant’s interior featuring a blue tarp backdrop and a menu board displaying seafood options, while the man continues to relish his meal with evident delight.

The text 'DAY 09: SEOUL' appears on screen. He continues to speak, smiling and waving, with a red bus and a black car passing behind him. The scene shifts to a first-person view from inside a moving car, showing the side mirror and a passing green landscape. The video then cuts to a digital map of South Korea, highlighting a route from Seoul to Sokcho-si, with a red pin marking the destination. The final shot is a first-person view of the man stepping out of the car onto a sidewalk, his blue jeans and white sneakers visible as he exits the vehicle.

segments.caption: The video opens with the Man in black shirt with 'i travel for food' standing beside a silver van, smiling and gesturing enthusiastically to the camera as he walks through a bustling urban street in Seoul, South Korea. He wears a black t-shirt with the white text 'i travel for food' and carries a black backpack. The city environment is lively, with buildings, parked cars, and pedestrians visible in the background under bright daylight. He continues walking, speaking directly to the camera with expressive hand gestures, conveying excitement. The scene transitions to a close-up of a steaming stone pot soup, garnished with green onions, julienned radish, and sesame seeds, indicating a traditional Korean dish. The camera then cuts to the exterior of a restaurant named '강원정 삼계탕' (Gangwon-jeong Samgye-tang), with a sign displaying business hours and a price of 17,000 won for chicken soup with ginseng. Inside, the man is seated at a wooden table, surrounded by side dishes including kimchi, green chili peppers, and radish, as he eagerly digs into the hot soup with a spoon, his face showing delight. The restaurant has a casual, traditional Korean ambiance with wooden doors and other diners in the background. The video captures his enthusiastic reaction to the food, emphasizing his passion for culinary exploration.

He is seen smiling, gesturing, and eating with visible enthusiasm.

The sign also notes that the restaurant is closed from 2:30 PM to 5:30 PM for dinner preparation.

segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of a steaming stone pot soup, likely samgye-tang (ginseng chicken soup), garnished with green onions and julienned radish, placed on a wooden table in a traditional Korean restaurant. The Man in black shirt with 'i travel for food' is seated at the table, actively engaging with the meal. He uses chopsticks and a spoon to carefully lift a large piece of tender chicken from the pot, revealing the rich, clear broth and the glistening, cooked rice inside the chicken cavity. He then scoops up a spoonful of the rice and broth, bringing it to his mouth with evident enjoyment. The camera captures his expressive facial reactions as he savors the dish, gesturing with his hands while speaking to the camera, likely describing the flavor and texture. The table is set with various Korean side dishes, including kimchi, green chili peppers, and radish, along with a glass of beer. In the background, other diners are visible, including a Woman in blue shirt and a Woman in striped shirt, contributing to the lively, casual atmosphere of the restaurant. The lighting is warm and natural, emphasizing the steam rising from the hot soup and the vibrant colors of the food. The scene conveys a sense of authentic, communal dining and the man’s genuine enthusiasm for the traditional Korean cuisine.

segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of a large metal pan filled with a hearty Korean stew, featuring chunks of meat, kimchi, and vegetables, being seasoned with red pepper flakes and sesame seeds by a woman in a green apron. The pan is then placed on a blue plastic strainer, and a rich, brown broth is ladled over the ingredients, causing steam to rise as the dish simmers. The camera captures the sizzling and bubbling of the stew as it cooks, emphasizing the vibrant colors and textures. The scene transitions to a man wearing a black t-shirt with the text 'i travel for food' printed on it, seated at a table in a casual restaurant kitchen. He gestures excitedly towards the steaming pot, then uses chopsticks to lift a piece of meat, showcasing its tenderness and the thick, savory sauce clinging to it. He takes a bite, expressing visible delight, and continues to eat with enthusiasm, surrounded by various side dishes including kimchi, pickled radish, and a plate of pancakes. The background reveals a busy kitchen with a woman in a green apron working, a refrigerator labeled 'UNICOLA UNIHiTECH', and a menu board with Korean text on the wall. The atmosphere is warm and inviting, with the focus on the communal enjoyment of the meal and the rich, aromatic stew.

segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of a rich, saucy Korean dish being stirred with a spoon, revealing tender, shredded meat and vegetables in a glossy, reddish-brown broth, suggesting a spicy stew or braised dish. The scene quickly transitions to a bustling urban street in Seoul, with cars, including a yellow taxi and a silver van, moving along a wide road lined with modern buildings and Korean signage. The camera then shifts to a first-person view from inside a moving car, showing the dashboard and a busy cityscape with traffic and greenery. The journey continues through the city, passing a traditional Korean gate with a multi-tiered roof, juxtaposed against modern high-rises, before arriving at a crowded, vibrant shopping street at night, illuminated by neon signs and packed with pedestrians. The scene then moves to a narrow alleyway at night, where a small restaurant with a glowing sign reading '서갈비' (Seo Galbi) is visible. The camera enters the restaurant, revealing a rustic interior with brick walls and several large, cylindrical metal grills, each with a central fire pit. A man wearing a black t-shirt with the text 'i travel for food' and a black backpack enters, smiling and speaking to the camera, clearly excited. He gestures toward the grills, which are actively cooking, and the on-screen text identifies the location as 'Myeongdong Seoseo Galbi, Seoul, South Korea'. The man continues to speak animatedly, his expression enthusiastic, as he explores the restaurant's unique cooking setup.

segments.caption: The video opens in a bustling, industrial-style kitchen or food stall, where a woman in a black apron and purple gloves carries a large white plate piled high with thinly sliced, marbled raw beef, garnished with green onions and sesame seeds. She approaches a circular grill with glowing coals beneath a metal grate, where small metal bowls of side dishes, including green peppers and a red sauce, are arranged. Using tongs, she carefully places the beef slices onto the hot grill, where they begin to sizzle and smoke. The camera focuses closely on the meat as it cooks, capturing the steam and the browning edges. The chef flips the meat with tongs, revealing a juicy, pink interior, and then places a piece into a small metal bowl containing a simmering broth with radish and green onions. The scene shifts to show three men gathered around the grill: the Man in black shirt with 'i travel for food' (wearing a black t-shirt with Thai text 'ไม่เผ็ด ไม่กิน'), a man in a light blue t-shirt, and a man in a black Nike t-shirt with a black backpack. They are all using chopsticks to eat the freshly grilled meat, expressing delight and excitement as they chew and gesture animatedly. The atmosphere is lively and communal, with the grill's flames and smoke adding to the dynamic, sensory-rich environment.

segments.caption: The video opens in a bustling, industrial-style Korean barbecue restaurant with exposed brick walls and stainless steel counters. The Man in black shirt with 'i travel for food' is joined by two companions: a Man in light blue t-shirt and a Man in black Nike t-shirt. They stand around a large, circular grill with glowing coals, where marbled beef is sizzling. The Man in black shirt with 'i travel for food' gestures animatedly while holding chopsticks, and the Man in light blue t-shirt picks up a green chili pepper, examining it before taking a bite. The Man in black Nike t-shirt, wearing a black backpack, also eats a chili pepper and laughs. The group then raises small glasses of golden soju in a toast, drinking together with smiles. The camera zooms in on the juicy, charred beef on the grill, then pulls back to an overhead shot showing the grill surrounded by small bowls of side dishes like kimchi, green onions, and sauces. The scene transitions to a dimly lit alleyway at night, where a neon sign for '서갈비' (Seo Galbi) glows red and white. The Man in black shirt with 'i travel for food' then appears in a brightly lit underground parking garage, speaking directly to the camera with a wide smile, gesturing with his hands. He wears a black backpack and a black t-shirt with 'i travel for food' printed in white. He walks through the garage, passing parked cars, including a white SUV, and continues talking enthusiastically to the camera, ending with a close-up of his smiling face as he points toward the camera.

segments.caption: The video begins in a dimly lit underground parking garage where a silver van is parked, its headlights on, and a man in a black t-shirt with 'i travel for food' printed on it steps out, smiling at the camera. He is wearing a black backpack and blue jeans, and he gestures excitedly as he walks toward the camera. The scene transitions to a dark, neon-lit street at night, where the man walks past a glowing arcade machine and a brightly lit restaurant with Korean signage, including 'EDIVA COFFEE' and '화모식당'. He then enters a modern, spacious lobby with a large green inflatable dinosaur mascot and a digital screen displaying 'WELCOME TO BANA CITY'. The man, now pulling a suitcase, walks through the lobby and into an elevator. He arrives at a modern hotel room with warm lighting, a small kitchenette, and a large window. He drops his suitcase, sits on a white armchair, and speaks enthusiastically to the camera, gesturing around the room. He then moves to the window, revealing a stunning nighttime cityscape with twinkling lights stretching into the distance, reflecting in the glass. The camera pans across the view, capturing the urban expanse below, as the man continues to speak, clearly delighted by the view.

segments.caption: The video opens with a dimly lit hotel room at night, where a camera setup is visible on a desk, reflecting the city lights outside the window. The scene transitions to a man in a black t-shirt with 'i travel for food' printed on it, smiling and speaking to the camera in the same room. The view then shifts to a breathtaking nighttime cityscape of Seoul, showcasing illuminated skyscrapers and a bridge over a river. A digital map of South Korea appears, highlighting Seoul with a red pin and bold text. The scene changes to a rainy street in Seoul, with water droplets on the pavement and a blue car driving by, accompanied by on-screen text 'DAY 10: SEOUL'. The man reappears, now outside in the rain, wearing the same black t-shirt and a backpack, smiling and talking to the camera. The video then cuts to close-up shots of sizzling grilled meat, followed by a fire pit with glowing coals and a grill being lifted, revealing raw meat being prepared for cooking. The final shot shows a person in black gloves handling raw meat near the grill, emphasizing the preparation of a traditional Korean barbecue meal.

segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of a chef's gloved hands slicing a thick, marbled cut of raw beef on a dark wooden cutting board, emphasizing the rich red color and intricate fat marbling. The scene transitions to a dark, rustic outdoor or semi-enclosed cooking area where the beef is placed on a metal wire grill suspended over a roaring fire pit fueled by dry straw. The flames lick the meat, creating intense heat and smoke, and the chef, wearing a black cap and face mask, uses tongs to adjust the grill. The fire is stoked with more straw, causing a dramatic burst of flames that engulf the grill. After the intense searing, the meat is removed and placed on a large, dark stone pot, where it is further cooked and then sliced with a sharp knife, revealing a juicy, tender interior. The final shot shows the cooked beef being served on a plate, garnished with garlic and other ingredients, ready to be eaten. The atmosphere is smoky and warm, with the fire providing the primary light source, creating a dramatic and appetizing visual narrative of traditional Korean fire-grilled meat preparation.

segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of a large, round, dark metal grill where thick cuts of marbled beef are being expertly cooked. A person wearing black gloves uses tongs to flip and rearrange the sizzling meat, which is accompanied by whole garlic cloves and pieces of zucchini placed around the edges of the grill. The beef develops a rich, caramelized crust as it cooks, with visible steam and fat rendering. The camera zooms in to capture the juicy, pink interior of the meat as it is cut into smaller, bite-sized pieces, revealing its tender texture. The scene then pulls back to reveal the Man in black shirt with 'i travel for food' seated at the table, watching intently and reacting with excitement as the cook continues to prepare the dish. The table is laden with an array of Korean side dishes, including kimchi, fresh greens, and dipping sauces, arranged in small white bowls. The man gestures with his hands, expressing his anticipation and delight, while the cook continues to manage the grill, ensuring the meat is cooked to perfection. The atmosphere is warm and inviting, with soft lighting and a focus on the communal, interactive nature of the meal. The final shots emphasize the glistening, perfectly cooked beef piled high on the grill, ready to be enjoyed.

segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of a sizzling, glistening dish of grilled meat, likely pork belly, being cooked on a hot grill, with steam rising and oil bubbling, emphasizing the rich, savory texture. The scene transitions to a dimly lit, cozy restaurant where three people are seated around a wooden table, enjoying a meal together. The Man in black shirt with 'i travel for food' is actively eating with chopsticks, engaging in conversation with the Man in blue shirt and the Woman in striped shirt, who is also eating and occasionally using her phone. The atmosphere is warm and convivial, with soft lighting from hanging lamps and exposed copper pipes on the ceiling adding to the rustic charm. The camera captures their interactions, including laughter and gestures, as they share food from a central grill. The scene then shifts to an exterior shot of a rainy city street, with a man walking under an umbrella, passing a white van parked on the wet pavement, creating a moody, atmospheric transition. The video cuts to the interior of a restaurant named 'Pro Soy Crab' in Seoul, South Korea, as indicated by on-screen text. The walls are adorned with autographs and messages from celebrities, including Super Junior members, displayed in a grid of red-bordered cards. The camera focuses on a large, steamed crab dish, its shell cracked open to reveal vibrant orange roe and succulent white meat, being served on a white plate. A hand wearing a clear plastic glove carefully lifts a piece of the crab, showcasing the rich, glistening interior, while side dishes like pickled vegetables and rice are visible in the background, completing the scene of a luxurious seafood meal.

segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of a man lying in bed, smiling warmly at the camera, suggesting a relaxed and personal start to the day. The scene quickly transitions to a close-up of a black camera bag being zipped shut, indicating preparation for travel or an outing. The next shot shows a person’s hand placing a black backpack onto a suitcase, reinforcing the theme of packing and departure. The setting shifts to a modern, spacious lobby with a large, green inflatable mascot, likely at a hotel or airport, where the man in the blue shirt with 'i travel for food' is seen smiling and speaking to the camera, establishing his role as the vlogger. The scene then cuts to a car interior, where a smartphone mounted on the dashboard displays a navigation app, showing a 2.0 km route, as the driver navigates through traffic. The video then moves to a restaurant setting, where the man in the blue shirt is seated at a wooden table, enthusiastically eating a steaming bowl of spicy stir-fry dish with a spoon, his face expressing delight. He is joined by a woman in a gray shirt, who is also eating, and the table is filled with various Korean dishes, including a metal bowl of rice and side dishes. The man continues to enjoy his meal, using chopsticks to pick up a piece of spicy, red-coated meat, likely kimchi or marinated pork, and brings it close to the camera, showcasing its texture and color. He then takes a bite, savoring the flavor, and gestures with his hand, possibly expressing approval. The camera focuses on the empty stone pot, now overturned, as the man scrapes the last bits of food from the pot with a spoon, emphasizing his enjoyment and the meal's completion. The final shot is a wide view from a moving vehicle, showing a scenic landscape with green fields, a bridge, and a distant airplane in the sky, suggesting a journey to a new destination, possibly an airport or rural area, under a cloudy sky.

segments.caption: The video opens with a wide shot of the Incheon International Airport arrival hall, showcasing a modern, spacious terminal with a large digital flight board displaying international arrivals, travelers with luggage, and signage in Korean and English, including 'International Arrivals' and gate information. The scene transitions to the exterior of the airport, where a man in a black t-shirt with 'i travel for food' printed on it is seen walking with a suitcase, greeting another man in a white t-shirt with a kangaroo logo and the word 'KANGOL' printed on it. They exchange a friendly handshake, and the man in the black shirt smiles warmly. The camera then follows the man in the black shirt as he walks through the airport, passing by a woman in a striped shirt who is holding a phone and a suitcase. The scene shifts to the interior of the airport, where the man in the black shirt is now seated on a teal bench, talking to the camera. He is wearing a blue t-shirt with the text 'i travel for food' printed on it, and he gestures animatedly as he speaks. The background shows a large, modern terminal with high ceilings, large windows, and a few other travelers. The man continues to speak, and a graphic overlay appears on the screen, showing his social media handle '@MarkWiens' and a 'Subscribed' button, indicating that he is a content creator. The video concludes with the man still seated, smiling and talking to the camera, with the airport terminal visible behind him.
